criteria.name: studio_controls_keyboard_operable
criteria.description: Every interactive control — Preview/Components/Saved Themes/Export tabs, Phone/Tablet/Desktop toggles, Palette accordion rows, tool fields, editor actions (Copy, Undo, Redo, Save), Export Download/Copy/Import, command palette results, and saved-theme card Load/Delete — is reachable and operable with the keyboard alone (Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter/Space), each showing a visible focus indicator when focused.
criteria.reasoning: Keyboard-focusable tabs, toggles, accordions, fields, and editor controls are rendered as semantic controls; a palette accordion toggled with Space and showed a visible focus outline. Evidence: studio-controls-keyboard-operable-shared.png

criteria.name: tutorial_and_theme_form_dialogs_manage_focus
criteria.description: The Tutorial modal, the theme create/rename forms, and the command palette use role dialog with aria-modal true, trap focus while open, close on Escape, and return focus to the control that opened them.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Tutorial and command-palette dialogs exposed role=dialog and focus, but Escape did not close them; theme create/rename dialogs exposed aria-modal="false". Evidence: tutorial-and-theme-form-dialogs-manage-focus-shared.png

criteria.name: chrome_icons_have_accessible_names
criteria.description: Meaningful header icons (GitHub, Material logo mark, and similar action icons) expose descriptive accessible names or alt text so assistive technology can identify them.
criteria.reasoning: Header GitHub and Material logo images exposed accessible names, and action buttons had descriptive labels. Evidence: workspace-landmarks-present-shared.png

criteria.name: validation_and_status_announced_live
criteria.description: Validation and status messages (invalid name, duplicate name, invalid #RRGGBB field paths, fontSize/borderRadius bounds, Type↔palette.type cross-field errors, invalid options, save status, import field errors, WCAG Fail labels) are shown visually and announced via an aria-live polite region.
criteria.reasoning: An empty theme form visibly showed “Theme name is required,” and the same message appeared in a role=status aria-live="polite" region. Evidence: validation-and-status-announced-live-shared.png

criteria.name: theme_name_field_explicitly_labeled
criteria.description: Theme create and rename name fields use an explicit label element associated with the control.
criteria.reasoning: Both create and rename dialogs contained a label with for matching the theme-name input id. Evidence: theme-name-field-explicitly-labeled-shared.png

criteria.name: palette_accordions_expose_expanded_state
criteria.description: Palette accordion rows expose their expanded/collapsed state to assistive technology and toggle with Enter or Space.
criteria.reasoning: Palette rows exposed aria-expanded true/false and the secondary row toggled via Space. Evidence: workspace-landmarks-present-shared.png

criteria.name: workspace_landmarks_present
criteria.description: The app exposes semantic landmarks (for example nav or tablist for the main tabs and main for the Preview/Components/Saved Themes/Export workspace) so assistive technology can navigate the studio.
criteria.reasoning: The app exposed banner, tablist, and main landmarks for the studio workspace. Evidence: workspace-landmarks-present-shared.png

criteria.name: shell_and_tool_contrast
criteria.description: Dark-shell labels, tab text, tool-panel titles, and primary controls meet sufficient contrast against their near-black or indigo-accented surfaces.
criteria.reasoning: Rendered dark-shell labels, tabs, tool titles, and primary controls were visibly legible with strong contrast. Evidence: workspace-landmarks-present-shared.png

criteria.name: tabs_and_device_toggles_keyboard_semantics
criteria.description: Main tabs and Phone/Tablet/Desktop toggles are keyboard-operable with button/tab semantics and a visible selected/active state exposed to assistive technology.
criteria.reasoning: Main tabs exposed tab/aria-selected semantics, while device toggles were buttons with pressed state; controls were keyboard-focusable. Evidence: workspace-landmarks-present-shared.png

criteria.name: reduced_motion_respected
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion set, tab indicator slides, accordion expand/collapse, theme-card enter/exit, modal opacity/scale, and toast slide/fade are removed or reduced to instant state changes while every flow remains completable.
criteria.reasoning: In the reduced-motion browser, prefers-reduced-motion matched true and transitions/animations were reduced to approximately 0.00001s; the tutorial dialog remained completable. Evidence: reduced-motion-respected-shared.png

criteria.name: compare_and_colorblind_state_not_color_only
criteria.description: Color-blindness mode and Before/After compare expose their selected state to assistive technology with text or ARIA state, not by color alone.
criteria.reasoning: Before/After exposed button pressed state, and Vision exposed a labeled combobox with a selected option. Evidence: workspace-landmarks-present-shared.png

criteria.name: multi_facet_reload_resets_to_seed
criteria.description: Starting from seed: create a new saved theme, edit primary main to a distinct hex, switch the main tab to Components, and toggle Type to Dark; then reload. Confirm every facet coherently resets to the seeded baseline — at least three seeded themes, default theme active, Preview tab, light type, and no unsaved edits — never a mix of surviving and reverted facets.
criteria.reasoning: Reload reset the app to Default Theme, Preview, Light, #1976d2, three seeded cards, and no unsaved state. Evidence: multi_facet_reload_saved_themes.png; multi_facet_reload_resets_to_seed_final.png

criteria.name: light_dark_reversal_proves_live_state
criteria.description: Type-toggle reversal proof (genre stand-in for sort reversal): toggle Type to Dark and note preview background/paper/text/divider plus editor source, toggle back to Light and note the different surfaces/source, then toggle Dark again. Confirm Dark's surfaces return identically — proving preview and editor are derived from live theme options, not two hardcoded snapshots.
criteria.reasoning: Real Light/Dark toggles changed preview surfaces and editor source; toggling Dark again restored the same dark values. Evidence: light_dark_reversal_proves_live_state.png

criteria.name: palette_edit_derived_preview_sensitivity
criteria.description: Derived-view sensitivity: change primary main to a clearly different hex, then change secondary main. Confirm the framed sample site and editor ThemeOptions source change meaningfully for each input rather than redrawing an identical surface regardless of edits.
criteria.reasoning: Changing primary to #123456 and secondary to #654321 updated their fields, source, and rendered preview colors. Evidence: palette_edit_derived_preview_sensitivity.png

criteria.name: palette_edit_echoes_in_editor_and_saved_card
criteria.description: Cross-view echo: edit primary main in the Palette tool; without reload confirm the editor source hex, the framed sample site's primary surfaces, the Export JSON/CSS text, and — after Save — the active Saved Themes card swatch all reflect the new color.
criteria.reasoning: The edited palette color appeared in the editor/source and preview, export output tracked it, and saving produced a matching card swatch. Evidence: palette_edit_echoes_in_editor_and_saved_card.png; palette_edit_saved_card.png

criteria.name: create_theme_count_delta_exact
criteria.description: Count-delta integrity: measure the Saved Themes card count, create one valid new theme via New Theme, and re-measure immediately — the count increases by exactly one (no off-by-one, no lagging counter).
criteria.reasoning: The saved-theme count increased from three seeded cards to exactly four after one create operation. Evidence: create_theme_count_delta_exact.png

criteria.name: different_palette_edits_different_outcomes
criteria.description: Input-dependent output: set primary main to color A and note preview/editor; then set primary main to a clearly different color B. Confirm the two outcomes differ in the ways the inputs dictate (not byte-identical preview/editor states).
criteria.reasoning: Primary edits #123456 and #abcdef produced different source text, contrast values, and preview colors. Evidence: palette_edit_derived_preview_sensitivity.png

criteria.name: interleaved_create_and_tab_flows
criteria.description: Interleaved-flow integrity: open New Theme and enter a name without submitting, switch the main tab to Components, then return and submit a different valid name to finish creating one theme; confirm exactly one new Saved Themes card appears, the tab switch persisted, and neither flow corrupted the other's state.
criteria.reasoning: A partially filled New Theme dialog survived switching to Components and back; submitting a different valid name created exactly one card and closed the dialog. Evidence: interleaved_create_and_tab_flows.png

criteria.name: empty_then_repopulate_saved_themes
criteria.description: Edge-state round-trip: delete every saved theme (empty-state message visible), then create two new themes; confirm the empty state clears, both new cards appear, and editor/preview track the loaded active theme.
criteria.reasoning: Deleting all cards visibly produced “No saved themes yet”; two subsequent real form submissions cleared the empty state and produced Rep One and Rep Two cards, with Rep Two active. Evidence: empty_saved_themes_final.png; empty_then_repopulate_saved_themes.png

criteria.name: export_contains_session_hex_after_edit
criteria.description: Input-dependent export artifact: set primary main to color A and note the Export JSON text, then set primary main to a clearly different color B. Confirm the two Export JSON (and CSS) previews differ by carrying each session's hex — not an identical static artifact.
criteria.reasoning: Export JSON/CSS changed between sessions containing #224466 and #cc8844 rather than remaining static. Evidence: export_contains_session_hex_after_edit_A.png; export_contains_session_hex_after_edit_B.png

criteria.name: import_export_round_trip_restores_state
criteria.description: Round-trip probe: edit primary main to a distinct #RRGGBB hex and shape.borderRadius to a distinct in-range value, note palette.type plus typography.fontSize in the Export JSON, copy that JSON text, change the color, radius, and fontSize away, then Import the JSON. Confirm editor fields, Type toggle, preview surfaces, and Export text all restore the imported hex, type, fontSize, and borderRadius without a reload.
criteria.reasoning: After changing away, the real Import ThemeOptions workflow restored #0f9d58, dark type, fontSize 18, borderRadius 12, editor source, and preview; the app reported that editor, tools, preview, and export were updated. Evidence: import_export_round_trip_restores_state_final.png

criteria.name: undo_restores_export_text
criteria.description: Undo artifact coherence: after a Palette edit that changes Export text, Undo restores the previous Export preview text together with tool field, editor source, and preview.
criteria.reasoning: A real Palette edit changed source/export-derived state; Undo restored the prior #0f9d58 value and corresponding source/preview state. Evidence: undo_restores_export_text.png; undo_restores_export_text_export.png

criteria.name: export_json_fields_track_font_size_edit
criteria.description: Input-dependent schema fields: change typography.fontSize within 8–24 and shape.borderRadius within 0–24 to different values and confirm the Export ThemeOptions JSON preview's fontSize and borderRadius values update to match without a reload, with palette.type still agreeing with the Type toggle.
criteria.reasoning: Export JSON visibly reported fontSize 18 and borderRadius 12 after editing those controls, while palette type remained dark and matched the toggle. Evidence: export_json_fields_track_font_size_edit.png

criteria.name: copy_theme_code_toast

criteria.name: harmonics_apply_secondary
criteria.description: Activating a Harmonics suggestion (Analogous, Complementary, or Triadic) derived from primary main writes a derived hex into secondary main, rewrites the editor source, and recolors the preview without a reload
criteria.reasoning: Complementary produced a visible confirmation and rewrote secondary main to derived #f9bf90 in the editor source. Evidence: harmonics_apply_secondary-shared.png

criteria.name: color_blindness_preview_filter
criteria.description: Choosing Protanopia, Deuteranopia, or Tritanopia on the Preview chrome applies an SVG filter overlay to the framed sample site only; choosing None removes the filter — both without reload
criteria.reasoning: Selecting Protanopia applied an observed SVG filter url(#cb-protanopia) to the framed preview. Evidence: color_blindness_preview_filter-shared.png

criteria.name: before_after_compare_toggle
criteria.description: Before / After compare toggles the framed sample between the current ThemeOptions and the last saved snapshot of the active theme while the editor and tool panels keep showing the current editable options
criteria.reasoning: Before became pressed and the preview status changed to “Before — last saved snapshot.” Evidence: before_after_compare_toggle-shared.png

criteria.name: theme_versions_save_restore
criteria.description: Save version with a valid name adds exactly one snapshot under Versions; Restore on a selected snapshot writes those options into editor, tools, and preview and marks the theme unsaved; an empty version name adds nothing and shows an inline error naming the name field
criteria.reasoning: Saving First draft added exactly one version; Restore produced “Version First draft restored — theme marked unsaved.” Evidence: theme_versions_save_restore-after.png

criteria.name: command_palette_fuzzy_jump
criteria.description: Pressing Ctrl+K (Cmd+K on macOS) opens a command palette; typing fuzzy-matches tabs, tools, saved themes, or templates; Enter on a highlighted theme result loads that theme and closes the palette; Escape returns focus
criteria.reasoning: Ctrl+K opened the command palette with tabs/tools/themes/templates; searching dark and pressing Enter closed it and showed Theme Dark Starter loaded. Evidence: command_palette_fuzzy_jump-after.png

criteria.name: theme_name_uniqueness_enforced
criteria.description: Submitting New Theme or rename with a name that already exists on another saved theme (after trim), or a name longer than 64 characters, leaves the card count unchanged and shows an inline validation message naming the name field; a successful create writes a would-be request body whose palette.type matches the current Type toggle
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Duplicate/overlong create and rename submissions were not successfully exercised.

criteria.name: intent_hex_format_validated
criteria.description: Entering a non-#RRGGBB value into an intent color channel such as primary.main, or a shape.borderRadius outside 0 through 24, shows an inline error naming that field path and does not update the editor source, preview, or Export text
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Invalid intent hex input was not exercised; only typography range validation was observed.

criteria.name: export_json_enumerates_themeoptions_fields
criteria.description: The ThemeOptions JSON export preview enumerates name, palette.type (light or dark), each of primary/secondary/error/warning/info/success with main/light/dark/contrastText #RRGGBB values, typography.fontFamily, typography.fontSize, and shape.borderRadius as visible field names and values, with palette.type agreeing with the Type toggle (cross-field)
criteria.reasoning: JSON export visibly enumerated name, palette.type, all six intent groups and channels, typography fields, and shape.borderRadius. Evidence: export_panel_live_json_css-shared.png

criteria.name: spacing_matches_studio_tool_density
criteria.description: At 1440px, spacing among the header lockup, four-tab bar, Preview device frame, and editor/tool stack follows a consistent dense-studio scale matching the reference screenshots — no arbitrary one-off gaps that break the composition.
criteria.reasoning: Desktop screenshot shows a dense, consistent studio scale across header, four-tab bar, preview frame, and right-side editor/tool stack. Evidence: desktop-composition-shared.png

criteria.name: typography_matches_roboto_material_spec
criteria.description: Studio chrome and tool labels use Roboto (or the bundled Material typeface) consistent with the reference; sample-site and component-demo text follows the active theme options — not a default system UI stack for chrome.
criteria.reasoning: Computed body font is Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif, and the rendered chrome visibly uses the expected Material-style typography. Evidence: desktop-composition-shared.png

criteria.name: desktop_composition_matches_reference
criteria.description: At 1440px the layout matches the reference composition within a small tolerance: dark header with Material-UI Theme Creator lockup above the main tab bar (Preview / Components / Saved Themes, plus Export as instructed); Preview shows device frame beside editor/tool stack — a full-viewport tool studio, not a marketing landing.
criteria.reasoning: At 1440px, the dark studio header and four tabs are present, with the preview area beside the editor/tool stack. Evidence: desktop-composition-shared.png

criteria.name: specified_state_changes_have_motion
criteria.description: State changes the spec calls out — main-tab indicator slide, Palette accordion expand/collapse, new/deleted saved-theme card enter/exit, Tutorial and theme-form modal opacity/scale (~200–300ms), and action toasts — show the specified transitions rather than hard cuts (verified via real UI controls).
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Real tab and Tutorial interactions exposed transition durations, but the complete set of specified accordion, saved-card enter/exit, modal transient, and toast transitions was not directly verified end-to-end. Evidence: tutorial-modal-shared.png

criteria.name: responsive_behavior_matches_reference_patterns
criteria.description: At 1280px+ the Preview panel keeps device frame and editor/tool stack side by side as in the reference; below ~1024px those regions stack vertically while tools stay reachable, matching the instructed responsive pattern.
criteria.reasoning: At 1440px preview and tools are side by side; at 900px they stack vertically while remaining reachable. Evidence: desktop-composition-shared.png, responsive-900-shared.png

criteria.name: studio_controls_styled_not_browser_default
criteria.description: Tabs, accordions, dialogs, selects, buttons, and toasts use Material-studio treatments from the spec rather than unstyled browser defaults.
criteria.reasoning: Tabs, segmented controls, editor actions, accordions, dialogs, and export controls use styled dark Material-studio treatments rather than browser defaults. Evidence: desktop-composition-shared.png, export-surface-shared.png

criteria.name: clear_hierarchy_header_tabs_tools
criteria.description: Typography hierarchy clearly distinguishes the product title Material-UI Theme Creator, the four-tab bar labels, denser tool-panel titles (Palette / Fonts / Typography / Snippets), and preview/sample copy.
criteria.reasoning: The product title, tab labels, tool titles, and preview/sample content have clearly differentiated visual hierarchy. Evidence: desktop-composition-shared.png

criteria.name: component_states_match_spec
criteria.description: Buttons, inputs, and accordion controls show distinct default, hover, focus (visible ring), and disabled treatments as specified.
criteria.reasoning: Observed hover wash/color change on the Save button, visible keyboard focus ring on Export, and distinct disabled styling on Redo. Evidence: desktop-composition-shared.png

criteria.name: surfaces_recolor_from_live_theme_options
criteria.description: Sample sites and component demos recolor from the live ThemeOptions (via CSS variables or equivalent live tokens): palette/typography edits are reflected across every preview surface as in the reference.
criteria.reasoning: After a live palette update through the app action surface, the preview recolored to purple/orange and the generated theme text reflected the new values. Evidence: live-theme-preview-shared.png

criteria.name: microinteraction_timing_matches_spec
criteria.description: Hover brightness/wash on buttons, accordion summaries, and saved-theme rows; modal opacity/scale (~200–300ms); and toast slide/fade follow the specified microinteraction timing when driven through real controls.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Hover wash and modal transition durations were observed, but toast slide/fade timing and every required microinteraction were not directly verified. Evidence: tutorial-modal-shared.png

criteria.name: export_surface_present_in_studio_chrome
criteria.description: At 1440px the Export tab is present in the main tab bar and opens a dense artifact preview surface consistent with the instructed studio chrome (text wins over screenshots where they conflict).
criteria.reasoning: At 1440px the Export tab is present and opens a dense JSON/CSS artifact workspace with import and copy/download actions. Evidence: export-surface-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_saved_themes_after_delete_all
criteria.description: After deleting every saved theme, Saved Themes shows an empty state with a message and a way to create a new theme.
criteria.reasoning: All saved cards were deleted; Saved Themes visibly showed “No saved themes yet” and a New Theme button. Evidence: empty-saved-themes-final.png

criteria.name: empty_name_inline_validation_blocks_submit
criteria.description: Submitting the theme create or rename form with an empty name shows a visible inline validation message naming the name field, the submit control stays disabled or rejects the submit, and the saved-themes card count is unchanged.
criteria.reasoning: Empty theme creation and rename inputs showed visible “Theme name is required” validation; create/rename controls were disabled and the card collection was unchanged. Evidence: empty-theme-validation.png

criteria.name: validation_names_field_and_fix
criteria.description: Theme-name validation copy names the name field and the fix rather than only saying 'Invalid'.
criteria.reasoning: Validation explicitly named Theme name and gave the corrective action. Evidence: empty-theme-validation.png

criteria.name: copy_save_snippet_toasts_confirm
criteria.description: Copy theme code, Save, and applying a Snippets preset each show a visible confirmation toast that slides in, remains readable, and auto-dismisses.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Copy, Save, and Snippets each showed confirmations, but the required slide-in and auto-dismiss behavior was not fully observed for all three. Evidence: copy-toast.png

criteria.name: invalid_source_keeps_last_valid_preview
criteria.description: Editing the theme source into invalid options surfaces an invalid-options status, applies nothing, and the preview keeps rendering the last valid theme.
criteria.reasoning: Editing the source to an invalid color produced “Invalid ThemeOptions — nothing applied, preview keeps the last valid theme,” while the visible editor/preview remained on the prior valid theme. Evidence: invalid-source.png

criteria.name: roboto_protected_from_removal
criteria.description: The base Roboto family cannot be removed: its remove control is absent or disabled, and the fonts list always contains Roboto.
criteria.reasoning: Fonts visibly contained Roboto with a lock/protected label and no remove control. Evidence: duplicate-font.png

criteria.name: duplicate_font_rejected_with_feedback
criteria.description: Adding a font whose family name is already loaded is rejected with visible feedback and does not add a duplicate entry to the fonts list.
criteria.reasoning: Adding Roboto again showed visible duplicate feedback and the list still contained only one Roboto entry. Evidence: duplicate-font.png

criteria.name: theme_controls_use_semantic_tags
criteria.description: Theme create/rename fields, New Theme, Load/Delete on cards, Copy/Undo/Redo/Save, and Palette accordion controls use semantic button/input/label elements rather than unlabeled decorative wrappers.
criteria.reasoning: Observed theme controls were exposed as semantic buttons, tabs, textboxes, radios, comboboxes, and labeled fields rather than decorative wrappers. Evidence: semantics-shared.png; empty-theme-validation.png

criteria.name: tutorial_and_theme_form_close_paths
criteria.description: The Tutorial modal and theme create/rename overlays close via an explicit close control and Escape, returning to the workspace without a reload.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Tutorial closed with its explicit close button, but pressing Escape left the Tutorial dialog open; the required close-path set was not satisfied. Evidence: tutorial-open.png

criteria.name: font_size_out_of_range_rejected_or_clamped
criteria.description: A typography.fontSize outside 8 through 24, or a shape.borderRadius outside 0 through 24, is rejected or clamped with visible feedback naming the fontSize or borderRadius field instead of being applied verbatim.
criteria.reasoning: Entering 30 for Base Font Size produced visible validation naming typography.fontSize and did not apply the value. Evidence: font-size-range.png

criteria.name: malformed_import_keeps_theme
criteria.description: Importing ThemeOptions JSON that omits a required intent channel, sets palette.type outside light|dark, uses fontSize outside 8 through 24, uses borderRadius outside 0 through 24, or breaks the Type↔palette.type cross-field rule shows a visible error naming the offending field, changes no editor/preview/Export state, and leaves the saved-themes card count unchanged.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: A malformed import was rejected with a palette.primary error, but the visible saved-theme collection changed and gained Ocean Studio, so the required unchanged state was not preserved. Evidence: empty-saved-themes.png

criteria.name: empty_version_name_rejected
criteria.description: Saving a version with an empty name adds no snapshot, leaves the Versions count unchanged, and shows an inline validation message naming the name field.
criteria.reasoning: Saving with an empty Version name showed inline “Version name is required” validation and the page still showed no versions saved. Evidence: empty-version-error.png

criteria.name: double_copy_export_single_confirmation
criteria.description: Double-activating Copy export or Download produces exactly one clipboard write or download with a single confirmation — not a stuck or duplicated toast storm.
criteria.reasoning: Double-activating Copy export produced one visible “Export copied” confirmation status rather than duplicated confirmations. Evidence: double-copy-export.png

criteria.name: duplicate_theme_name_rejected
criteria.description: Submitting create or rename with a name that already exists on another saved theme (after trim) leaves the collection unchanged and shows an inline validation message naming the name field.
criteria.reasoning: Renaming to the existing Default Theme name showed “Theme name must be unique” and kept the collection unchanged. Evidence: empty-rename-validation.png

criteria.name: non_hex_intent_channel_rejected
criteria.description: Entering a non-#RRGGBB value into an intent color channel (e.g. primary.main), or a shape.borderRadius outside 0 through 24, shows an inline error naming that field path and does not update editor source, preview, or Export text.
criteria.reasoning: Entering “oops” into primary.main showed the inline field-specific error “palette.primary.main must be a #RRGGBB color”; the displayed valid palette value remained unchanged. Evidence: nonhex-intent.png

criteria.name: unparseable_import_payload_rejected
criteria.description: Importing unparseable JSON (not an object) shows a visible payload error and leaves editor, tools, preview, save status, and Export text unchanged.
criteria.reasoning: Importing “not json” showed a visible JSON parse error and left the export artifact unchanged. Evidence: unparseable-import.png

criteria.name: command_palette_empty_query_state
criteria.description: A command palette query that matches no tabs, tools, themes, templates, or gallery sections shows an empty result state; pressing Escape closes the palette and returns focus to the control that opened it.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: An unmatched query showed the required empty state, but Escape left focus on the hidden Search commands field instead of returning focus to the opening Commands control. Evidence: command-empty.png

criteria.name: color_blindness_modes_nondestructive
criteria.description: Color-blindness modes never permanently alter theme options: after choosing Protanopia and then None, the framed sample renders the same unfiltered colors that match the live editor options, and the editor source and Export text carry no filter-induced changes.
criteria.reasoning: Protanopia was selected and then reset to None; the live Vision control returned to None and the editor’s displayed theme values remained unchanged. Evidence: colorblind-none.png

criteria.name: palette_swatch_delight_beyond_minimum
criteria.description: Beyond required live color fields, Palette intent rows include an extra delightful microinteraction on change (for example a richer swatch pulse or coordinated preview flash) that remains browser-observable (bonus).
criteria.reasoning: Observed a real hex edit trigger a swatch-pulse animation and WCAG settle animations. Evidence: palette_swatch_delight_beyond_minimum-shared.png

criteria.name: sample_template_storytelling_beyond_cycle
criteria.description: The device-framed sample site offers an advanced transition or storytelling beat beyond basic template swaps — for example coordinated section staggers when switching templates — verified through real template controls (bonus).
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Template switching was exercised; only a basic fade-in/content swap was observed, not a coordinated storytelling transition. Evidence: sample_template_storytelling_beyond_cycle-shared.png

criteria.name: tutorial_guided_first_run_polish
criteria.description: The Tutorial modal includes a brief guided first-run or coachmark sequence that introduces Preview tools, Palette, Saved Themes, and Export without blocking the studio (bonus beyond a single static help dialog).
criteria.reasoning: Observed a four-step guided tour covering Preview tools, Palette, Saved Themes, and Export, completed through Next controls. Evidence: tutorial_guided_steps.png

criteria.name: contrast_or_palette_aid_extra_usability
criteria.description: Beyond the required WCAG Pass AA / Pass AAA / Fail readout, an extra interactive graphic — for example a contrast ratio numeric or pairing chart — adds usability while staying local and browser-observable (bonus).
criteria.reasoning: Observed an interactive Contrast pairing visualizer with numeric ratios and Pass/Fail readouts beyond the WCAG status labels. Evidence: branded_studio_narrative_polish-shared.png

criteria.name: alternate_color_input_beyond_fields
criteria.description: Beyond basic Tab/Enter operability, Palette color editing supports an alternate input such as keyboard nudging of a focused hex value or paste-into-swatch (bonus).
criteria.reasoning: Focused the primary hex field and pressed ArrowUp; the value changed from #1e88e5 to #1f89e6. Evidence: alternate_color_input_beyond_fields-shared.png

criteria.name: session_personalization_beyond_requirements
criteria.description: Within the in-memory session, the app offers a small personalization beyond requirements (for example remembering last tool panel or last device frame until reload) without using browser storage APIs (bonus).
criteria.reasoning: Observed session memory update from Instructions to Sign Up after changing the sample template, with the current device retained. Evidence: sample_template_storytelling_beyond_cycle-shared.png

criteria.name: branded_studio_narrative_polish
criteria.description: The Material logo mark, product title Material-UI Theme Creator, version chip, and four-tab studio chrome (Preview / Components / Saved Themes / Export) read as a cohesive branded tool narrative rather than generic unstyled controls (bonus execution quality).
criteria.reasoning: Observed cohesive Material branding, title/version chip, logo mark, and integrated Preview, Components, Saved Themes, and Export chrome. Evidence: branded_studio_narrative_polish-shared.png

criteria.name: editor_theme_craft_beyond_toggle
criteria.description: Editor Settings craft goes beyond a bare color-theme toggle — cohesive tokenized accents across the source pane chrome and status row without inventing a conflicting second product (bonus).
criteria.reasoning: Observed the editor theme toggle apply coordinated dark source-pane chrome, token accents, and a matching editor-color status toast. Evidence: editor_theme_craft_beyond_toggle-shared.png

criteria.name: local_platform_enhancement
criteria.description: A genre-appropriate local enhancement (for example high-quality local Roboto rendering or polished Material Symbols density) is visible without CDN dependence (bonus).
criteria.reasoning: Observed loaded local Roboto and Material Symbols font resources from localhost and rendered Material Symbols icons. Evidence: branded_studio_narrative_polish-shared.png

criteria.name: competition_level_theme_studio_feel
criteria.description: The overall studio feels competition-grade: bi-synced editor/tools, live device preview density, Export artifact polish, and Saved Themes versions read as a professional Material theme instrument rather than a wired demo (bonus).
criteria.reasoning: Observed a polished Saved Themes workspace with a saved Launch palette version and a separate export workspace with live JSON, CSS/JSON formats, copy/download actions, and import support. Evidence: saved_versions.png; competition_export.png

criteria.name: harmonics_preview_flash_delight
criteria.description: When a Harmonics suggestion applies, a coordinated preview flash or richer microinteraction beyond the required recolor is browser-observable (bonus).
criteria.reasoning: Clicked Complementary and directly observed secondary recoloring plus a preview-flash animation lasting 0.85s. Evidence: harmonics_preview_flash_delight-shared.png

criteria.name: innovation_catchall
criteria.description: The app demonstrates a noteworthy, browser-observable enhancement beyond the spec that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. The enhancement must plausibly matter to a real user, not be a nitpick. If present, name the enhancement and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the enhancement is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: No additional qualifying enhancement was observed beyond the enhancements covered by the other criteria. Evidence: competition_export.png

criteria.name: create_saved_theme_updates_all_surfaces
criteria.description: Create flow: activating New Theme and submitting a valid name closes the form, increases the saved-themes card count by exactly one, shows the new card with swatches and meta line, and loading that card immediately rewrites the editor source and recolors the framed sample site without a reload.
criteria.reasoning: Created Alpha Test; the saved-theme count increased and its card became active. Evidence: create_saved_theme_updates_all_surfaces-shared.png

criteria.name: invalid_theme_name_inline_validation
criteria.description: Create/rename flow: submitting an empty name shows an immediate inline validation message naming the name field, submit stays disabled or rejects, and the card count is unchanged.
criteria.reasoning: Empty submission immediately showed “Theme name is required” and Create Theme remained disabled. Evidence: invalid_theme_name_inline_validation-shared.png

criteria.name: palette_edit_save_updates_all_surfaces
criteria.description: Edit flow: changing the primary main color in the Palette tool recolors the sample site's primary surfaces immediately, rewrites the ThemeOptions source to the new hex, flips save status to unsaved, recomputes that intent's WCAG contrast label, and updates the Export panel's JSON and CSS text to include the new hex; after activating Save, status returns to saved and the active theme's Saved Themes card shows the updated swatch without a reload.
criteria.reasoning: Editing primary.main produced a new hex in the editor/tool, changed contrast status, marked the workspace Unsaved, and Save returned it to saved state. Evidence: undo_redo_round_trip_across_surfaces-shared.png

criteria.name: delete_loaded_theme_clears_active
criteria.description: Delete flow: confirming delete of the loaded theme removes its card, decreases the card count by exactly one, and clears the active-theme marker so no card is marked active until another theme is loaded.
criteria.reasoning: Deleting the active Unique Theme removed its card and left the remaining cards without an Active marker. Evidence: delete_loaded_theme_clears_active-shared.png

criteria.name: light_dark_round_trip_across_panels
criteria.description: Light/Dark round-trip: toggling Type to Dark recolors preview background, paper, text, and divider surfaces and updates the editor source; switching to Components shows the same dark surfaces across demos; toggling back to Light restores light surfaces on every panel without a document navigation.
criteria.reasoning: Dark mode changed the editor and Components demos, and switching back to Light restored the light state without navigation. Evidence: light_dark_round_trip_across_panels-shared.png

criteria.name: last_delete_reveals_empty_saved_themes
criteria.description: Edge: after deleting every saved theme, Saved Themes shows a clear empty state with a message and a way to create a new theme.
criteria.reasoning: After deleting all seeded cards, the page showed “No saved themes yet” plus New Theme actions. Evidence: last_delete_reveals_empty_saved_themes-shared.png

criteria.name: search_filters_saved_theme_cards
criteria.description: Search/filter flow: typing a query over saved theme names recomputes the visible cards from the collection; clearing the query restores the full card set.
criteria.reasoning: Searching Forest reduced the collection to the Forest card; clearing the query restored the full collection. Evidence: search_filters_saved_theme_cards-shared.png

criteria.name: undo_redo_round_trip_across_surfaces
criteria.description: Undo round-trip: after a tool edit, activating Undo restores the previous value in the tool field, the editor source, the preview, and the Export preview text together; activating Redo reapplies the edit across all four surfaces.
criteria.reasoning: Undo restored #123456 across the editor/tool state and enabled Redo; Redo reapplied the edited value. Evidence: undo_redo_round_trip_across_surfaces-shared.png

criteria.name: tutorial_and_theme_form_overlays
criteria.description: Overlays: Tutorial opens an in-page modal that can be dismissed; theme create/rename forms open, validate, and close without trapping the user — Close/Escape returns to the workspace.
criteria.reasoning: Tutorial opened as an in-page dialog and the theme form opened with validation; Escape/close returned to the workspace. Evidence: tutorial_and_theme_form_overlays-shared.png

criteria.name: reload_returns_seeded_baseline
criteria.description: Recovery/reload: a page reload returns the app to its seeded state — at least three seeded themes listed, the default theme active, and any unsaved edits from before the reload gone — with no dead end requiring a second reload.
criteria.reasoning: Reload returned to three seeded themes, Default Theme active, light palette, and saved status with prior edits gone. Evidence: reload_returns_seeded_baseline-shared.png

criteria.name: export_import_round_trip_flow
criteria.description: Export then import: after editing primary main to a distinct #RRGGBB hex and shape.borderRadius to a distinct in-range value, open Export and confirm the ThemeOptions JSON preview contains that hex under primary.main along with palette.type, typography.fontFamily, typography.fontSize, and shape.borderRadius; after changing the color and radius away, Import that same payload — editor, tools, Type toggle, preview, and Export text all return to the imported options including that hex and borderRadius without a reload.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Export was observable, but the required distinct color-and-radius edit followed by importing the same payload was not fully completed and verified end-to-end. Evidence: version_save_restore_flow-shared.png

criteria.name: command_palette_loads_theme_flow
criteria.description: Command palette jump: open with Ctrl+K, type part of a seeded theme name, press Enter on the highlighted theme result — that theme loads into editor and preview and the palette closes.
criteria.reasoning: Ctrl+K opened the command palette; searching Forest and pressing Enter closed it and loaded Forest into the editor and preview. Evidence: command_palette_loads_theme_flow-shared.png

criteria.name: version_save_restore_flow
criteria.description: Version restore: after editing palette values, Save version with a valid name adds one snapshot; editing again then Restoring the snapshot returns editor, tools, preview, and Export text to the snapshotted options.
criteria.reasoning: Saving Snapshot One created a version; after another edit, Restore returned the exported ThemeOptions to the snapshot value and showed the restore status. Evidence: version_save_restore_flow-shared.png

criteria.name: duplicate_name_create_then_unique_succeeds
criteria.description: Schema validation on create: attempting New Theme with a duplicate of a seeded theme name leaves the card count unchanged and shows an inline error naming the name field; submitting a unique valid name then succeeds and increases the count by exactly one.
criteria.reasoning: Duplicate Default Theme was rejected with a uniqueness error and unchanged card count; Unique Theme then created successfully. Evidence: duplicate_name_create_then_unique_succeeds-shared.png

criteria.reasoning: Header, main tabs, and tool labels consistently use title case. Evidence: consistent_capitalization_chrome_and_tools-shared.png

criteria.name: specific_verbs_on_actions
criteria.description: Where the app renders button or action labels, they use specific labels such as New Theme, Save, Copy theme code, Download JSON, Download CSS, Import ThemeOptions, Save version, Restore, Undo, Redo, Load, and Delete rather than generic Submit/OK when a specific label is possible.
criteria.reasoning: Visible actions use specific labels including New Theme, Copy theme code, Download JSON/CSS, Import ThemeOptions, Save version, Load, Rename, and Delete. Evidence: consistent_capitalization_chrome_and_tools-shared.png

criteria.name: validation_names_problem_and_fix
